zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Holland於大約1280年出生在英國藍卡爾武普 霍蘭時，她的父親Sir Robert de Holand II36歲，母親Elizabeth de Salmesbury26歲。她於大約1304年與Sir Edmund Talbot在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1328年10月7日在英國英格蘭藍卡爾享年49歲，埋葬在英國可瓦爾博德明。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Holland于大约1280年出生在英国藍卡爾武普 霍蘭时，她的父亲Sir Robert de Holand II36岁，母亲Elizabeth de Salmesbury26岁。她于大约1304年与Sir Edmund Talbot在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1328年10月7日在英国英格兰藍卡爾享年49岁，埋葬在英国可瓦爾博德明。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Holland was born about 1280, in Up Holland, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Robert de Holand II, was 36 and her mother, Elizabeth de Salmesbury, was 26. She married Sir Edmund Talbot about 1304, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 7 October 1328, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49, and was buried in Bodmin, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Holland est née environ 1280, à Up Holland, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert de Holand II, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Salmesbury, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edmund Talbot environ 1304, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée le 7 octobre 1328, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Bodmin, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Holland um 1280 in Up Holland, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert de Holand II 36 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Salmesbury 26. Sie hat um 1304 in England, United Kingdom Sir Edmund Talbot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 7. Oktober 1328 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bodmin, Cornwall, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Holland è nata il circa 1280 nel Up Holland, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Robert de Holand II, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Salmesbury, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir Edmund Talbot circa 1304, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 7 ottobre 1328, nel Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolta a Bodmin, Cornwall, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Hollandが約1280年にイギリスランカシュアイアウップ ホーランドで生まれた時，父Sir Robert de Holand IIは36歳，母Elizabeth de Salmesburyは26歳であった。約1304年にイギリスイギリスでSir Edmund Talbotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1328年10月7日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアで49歳で死去し，イギリスコルンワルボドミンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Holland(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 랭카실르 업 홀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert de Holand II(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Salmesbury(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1304년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Edmund Talbot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1328년 10월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 코른월 보드민에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Holland nasceu aproximadamente 1280, em Up Holland, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert de Holand II, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Salmesbury, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edmund Talbot aproximadamente 1304, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 7 de outubro de 1328, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Bodmin, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Holland родилась около 1280 г. в местности уп холланд, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert de Holand II было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Salmesbury, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edmund Talbot около 1304 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 7 октября 1328 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности бодминь, корнвалл, Англия.

Se casó con Sir Edmund Talbot aproximadamente 1304, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 7 de octubre de 1328, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultada en Bodmin, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
